label: Institution type
content: Mutual savings bank — depositor-owned, no shareholders. 170+ year old community bank. Conservative, relationship-driven culture.

label: Founded
content: February 14, 1852, Bangor, Maine.

label: Total assets
content: ~$7 billion+

label: Branches
content: 60+ across Maine and New Hampshire.

label: Brand positioning
content: 'You Matter More' — strong community identity. Evaluating vendors through relationship and trust lens.

label: Core banking
content: Jack Henry jXchange — #1 integration risk area. Must be specific, honest, and detailed about what integration covers.

label: Fraud monitoring
content: Verafin

label: Credit bureau
content: TransUnion

label: Current card processor
content: Centrix DTS — being replaced. This is the migration target.

label: Customer engagement
content: Glia (digital messaging/video)

label: Document management
content: DocuSign

label: Online banking
content: Investing in digital transformation. Glia for digital engagement, modernized online banking.

label: Merchant acquiring
content: Global Payments/TSYS for merchant acquiring, Clover and Toast POS — separate from card issuing RFP.

label: Consumer card suite
content: 'Everblue' suite of consumer rewards credit cards.

label: Youth card
content: 'Pling' teen/youth card.

label: Business cards
content: Business credit cards.

label: Mastercard membership
content: Principal Mastercard membership — not using BIN sponsorship from another institution. Positive signal for BRIM.

label: No Canada/US framing
content: Never say 'Canadian and US requirements' or 'Canada and US' in BSB-facing content. Position from US perspective only.

label: Mutual savings bank language
content: Do not use shareholder-centric language. Frame value in terms of depositor benefit, community impact, and operational efficiency.

label: Examiner lens
content: Write as if OCC/FDIC examiner will read it during third-party risk review. Every answer must demonstrate BSB retains appropriate oversight.

label: Jack Henry integration — #1 risk
content: Integration quality determines daily operational experience. Bangor will weight this heavily. Verify engineering depth before committing specifics.

label: October 2027 migration deadline
content: Tight but achievable. Position with buffer for UAT and parallel run. Work backwards to show adequate runway.

label: Centrix DTS migration path
content: Need clear plan: cardholder data migration, open dispute transfer, rewards balance migration, communication plan.

label: Depositor governance
content: Depositor-owned. Frame value in terms of depositor benefit and community impact.

label: 1 — Jack Henry depth
content: Can BRIM integrate cleanly with jXchange? Highest weight factor.

label: 2 — Migration risk
content: How does BRIM de-risk transition from Centrix DTS?

label: 3 — Rewards flexibility
content: Can BRIM support and enhance the Everblue program?

label: 4 — Community bank fit
content: Is BRIM right-sized for a $7B mutual savings bank?

label: 5 — Regulatory comfort
content: Will Maine Bureau / FDIC be comfortable with this vendor?
